summary: Plan-only assessment: #90998 is an open, focused contributor PR with no security-sensitive signal, but it is not merge-ready from the hydrated artifact because mergeability is unknown, checks were not fetched due a GitHub API rate-limit error, comments/review-bot comments were not hydrated, and no Codex /review preflight is present. Recommend repairing/validating the contributor branch rather than merging or escalating to maintainer judgment.

actions.reason: The PR appears useful and narrow, but merge is blocked by missing proof required by merge policy. Because maintainer_can_modify=true and the repair surface is narrow, the actionable path is to repair/refresh the contributor branch, hydrate comments and review-bot findings, run Codex /review, and validate before any merge recommendation.
Create an executable repair artifact for the existing contributor branch so the executor can refresh, inspect, validate, and review it without replacing contributor work.

repair_candidate.summary: Repair and validate contributor PR #90998, which adds SMS text slash-command authorization and command metadata handling. The executor should keep the existing contributor branch as the source path, hydrate missing comments/review-bot findings, refresh against current main if needed, run targeted tests and Codex /review, then only recommend merge if all merge preflight gates pass.
repair_candidate.pr_body:
Clownfish repair path for source PR #90998 by @clawSean.

Summary:
- Preserve the contributor's SMS slash-command authorization fix.
- Refresh or minimally repair the contributor branch only if validation, review, or current-main compatibility requires it.
- Carry forward attribution to @clawSean and source PR https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/90998.

Validation:
- pnpm -s vitest run extensions/sms/src/inbound.test.ts
- pnpm check:changed
- Codex /review clean before merge consideration.
credit_notes: Preserve contributor credit for clawSean as author of source PR #90998.
Reference source PR https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/90998 in any repair commit, replacement PR body, and user-facing changelog entry if one is required.
